title: As the president touts Trump Accounts, some families are still waiting for money

title: Model recruiter who introduced young women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France

title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow sues his mother over his estate

title: Judge approves a $1.5B Anthropic settlement over books used to train Claude
summary: A federal judge has approved a $1.5 billion copyright settlement in which artificial intelligence company Anthropic will pay thousands of authors about $3,000 per book after using pirated copies of their works to train its Claude chatbot.

title: Maduro trial date set for June 2027 as drug trafficking case against ousted Venezuelan leader proceeds
summary: Ousted Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro appeared in a New York City court Wednesday as his drug trafficking case moves forward. A trial date was set for June 2027.

title: 2 men suspected in $96k vandalism of downtown St. Pete tower turn themselves in: SPPD
summary: Two men suspected of tearing out landscaping lights and shattering windows at a downtown St. Petersburg residential tower turned themselves in at the Pinellas County Jail.

title: Tesla launches driverless Robotaxi service in Tampa
summary: Tesla announced its driverless ride service will now be available in Tampa, according to a post from the company's X account.

title: Independent autopsy of Nolan Wells did not determine the cause or manner of his death
summary: A second, independent autopsy of 18-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells has not yet determined the cause or manner of his death, announced civil rights attorney Ben Crump, who is representing Wells' family.

title: 71% of middle-income Americans say their income can't keep up with cost of living, survey finds
summary: A Primerica survey finds 71% of middle-income Americans say their income can't keep up with rising costs.

title: PSTA to vote on settlement for Clearwater Ferry crash victims
summary: The Pinellas County Transit Authority will vote today on whether or not to approve a $300,000 settlement amount to be given to the victims of the Clearwater Ferry crash.

title: 8 arrests after 4-day sexual exploitation enforcement sting: Zephyrhills police
summary: The Zephyrhills Police Department announced Wednesday the arrest of eight people following a human trafficking crackdown.

title: After $102 million jewel theft, Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery with new focus
summary: Louvre reopens its Apollo Gallery months after a $102 million jewel heist, with tighter security and upgraded surveillance now in place.

title: An OpenAI test model escaped and broke into a real company’s servers
summary: OpenAI says some of its experimental AI models left a test environment with no human direction and hacked its way onto a different company’s real production systems while trying to “cheat” on a cybersecurity test.

title: Trump threatens 100 percent generic drug tariffs beginning in 2028
summary: Imported generic drugs will face a 100 percent tariff beginning in August 2028 unless manufacturers move production to the U.S., President Trump said in a social media post Tuesday. Trump wrote on Truth Social the move is aimed at "reshoring" generic pharmaceutical production in America. The tariffs amount to a "penalty" that would hit companies if they don't [...]

summary: While driverless vehicles like Waymo have become a familiar sight across the country, Tesla is now entering the market locally with the official launch of its Robotaxi service in Tampa and Orlando.

title: Brazil threatens reciprocal tariffs after US targets imports
summary: Brazil’s government blasted new U.S. tariffs as unfair and offensive, warning it could respond with reciprocal trade measures.

title: Reconciliation 3.0: What the GOP's $95 billion legislative push means for you and your family
summary: Congress votes on reconciliation 3.0, a $95B package covering the Iran War, farm aid, and election reform. Here's what it means for you.

title: Anna Maria Island turtle watch volunteers work to protect sea turtle nests on Florida beaches
summary: Sea turtle nesting season runs from May 1 through October 31, and on Anna Maria Island, volunteers are working every day to make sure every hatchling has the best chance of making it to the water.

summary: 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extended: Governor Signs Death Warrant for William Silvia as Two Other Death Row Inmates Await Execution first appeared on The Florida Channel The state has already executed ten death row inmates this year and has plans to execute three more.
